test_output_summary: 37 of 37 tests in tests/forge/gating/test_models.py PASS (Pydantic v2, Python 3.12). Surrounding suite verified: 420 of 420 collected tests across tests/forge/ (excluding NATS-bound pipeline_consumer/publisher/lifecycle suites that require nats-py runtime) also PASS. Ruff check + ruff format --check on the three new files: clean.
implementation_notes: Added forge.gating.models per DM-gating.md §1/§2/§3/§6. Defined GateMode (4 members) and ResponseKind (5 members) as str-Enum subclasses, plus PriorReference, DetectionFinding, GateDecision, CalibrationAdjustment, and a minimal placeholder ConstitutionalRule needed only to type the evaluate_gate signature (the detailed schema is owned by TASK-CGCP-004). All Pydantic models use ConfigDict(extra='forbid') and Field(..., description='...') consistent with forge.config.models / forge.discovery.models. GateDecision enforces the three §6 invariants in a model_validator(mode='after'): MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL requires auto_approve_override=True OR threshold_applied=None; coach_score is None forces mode in {FLAG_FOR_REVIEW, HARD_STOP, MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL}; criterion_breakdown values must lie in [0, 1]. evaluate_gate is a keyword-only stub (matches DM-gating §3 signature byte-for-byte) that raises NotImplementedError, leaving the body to TASK-CGCP-004 (constitutional override) and TASK-CGCP-005 (reasoning-model assembly). The package re-exports new symbols from __init__.py while preserving the existing derive_request_id export from TASK-CGCP-003. Imports are limited to stdlib (datetime, enum, typing) and pydantic—no nats_core, nats-py, langgraph, or forge.adapters.* anywhere in the package; an AST-based purity test enforces this.
concerns: ConstitutionalRule is referenced in the DM-gating §3 evaluate_gate signature but its detailed schema is not specified. I shipped a minimal placeholder (rule_id + description) so the stub signature type-checks; TASK-CGCP-004 will need to extend or replace it. This was the only way to satisfy AC-010's 'full keyword-only signature' requirement without inventing fields beyond the spec.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `GateMode` enum defined with four members (`AUTO_APPROVE`, `FLAG_FOR_REVIEW`, `HARD_STOP`, `MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL`) per `DM-gating.md §1`
completion_promises.evidence: GateMode(str, Enum) declared in src/forge/gating/models.py with exactly the four required members (values match names). Verified by TestGateModeEnum: test_gate_mode_has_exactly_four_members, test_gate_mode_values_match_names, test_gate_mode_is_str_enum.

PriorReference(BaseModel) declared with entity_id: str, group_id: Literal['forge_pipeline_history','forge_calibration_history'], summary: str, relevance_score: float | None (constrained to [0,1]). Tests verify required-field construction, optional relevance_score, rejection of unknown group_id literal values, and rejection of relevance_score outside the unit interval.

DetectionFinding(BaseModel) declared with pattern: str, severity: Literal['low','medium','high','critical'], evidence: str, criterion: str | None. TestDetectionFindingModel verifies field set, optional criterion, parametrised acceptance of all four severities, and rejection of an out-of-set severity.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `GateDecision` Pydantic model with all fields per `DM-gating.md §1` — invariants enforced by validators per §6
completion_promises.evidence: GateDecision(BaseModel) exposes every DM-gating §1 field: build_id, stage_label, target_kind (Literal), target_identifier, mode, rationale, coach_score, criterion_breakdown, detection_findings, evidence, threshold_applied, auto_approve_override, degraded_mode, decided_at. _check_invariants is a model_validator(mode='after') that enforces all three §6 invariants. test_gate_decision_exposes_every_dm_gating_field guards the field set; test_gate_decision_with_minimal_valid_kwargs_constructs covers the happy path.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `mode == MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL ⇒ auto_approve_override is True OR threshold_applied is None`
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented as the first branch of GateDecision._check_invariants. test_mandatory_mode_with_override_and_threshold_is_allowed and test_mandatory_mode_with_no_threshold_is_allowed cover the two valid configurations; test_mandatory_mode_with_threshold_and_no_override_is_rejected covers the violation.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `coach_score is None ⇒ mode in {FLAG_FOR_REVIEW, HARD_STOP, MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL}`
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented as the second branch of GateDecision._check_invariants (degraded mode cannot AUTO_APPROVE). test_degraded_mode_cannot_auto_approve covers the rejected case; parametrised test_degraded_mode_accepts_non_auto_approve_modes covers all three permitted modes.

Implemented as the third branch of GateDecision._check_invariants (collects offenders into a single ValueError). test_criterion_breakdown_accepts_unit_interval_values covers boundary values 0.0 and 1.0; test_criterion_breakdown_rejects_value_above_one and test_criterion_breakdown_rejects_negative_value cover violations.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `CalibrationAdjustment` Pydantic model with all fields per `DM-gating.md §1`
completion_promises.evidence: CalibrationAdjustment(BaseModel) declared with all DM-gating §1 fields: adjustment_id, target_capability, project_scope, observed_pattern, proposed_bias, approved_by_rich, approved_at, expires_at, supersedes. test_calibration_adjustment_exposes_every_dm_gating_field guards the field set; test_calibration_adjustment_with_all_fields_constructs and test_calibration_adjustment_minimal_construction cover full and minimal construction with the defaults from §1.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `ResponseKind` enum defined with five members per `DM-gating.md §2`
completion_promises.evidence: ResponseKind(str, Enum) declared with the five members ACCEPT_ALL, ACCEPT_WITH_EDIT, REJECT, DEFER, CUSTOM. test_response_kind_has_exactly_five_members and test_response_kind_is_str_enum verify the surface.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `evaluate_gate()` stub function with full keyword-only signature per `DM-gating.md §3` raises `NotImplementedError`
completion_promises.evidence: evaluate_gate(*, target_kind, target_identifier, stage_label, coach_score, criterion_breakdown, detection_findings, retrieved_priors, calibration_adjustments, constitutional_rules) -> GateDecision implemented as a NotImplementedError shell. test_evaluate_gate_signature_is_keyword_only_with_expected_params verifies parameter order and that every parameter is KEYWORD_ONLY; test_evaluate_gate_raises_not_implemented exercises the call; test_evaluate_gate_return_annotation_is_gate_decision resolves the annotation via typing.get_type_hints to confirm the GateDecision return type.

models.py imports only datetime, enum, typing.Literal, and pydantic. __init__.py imports only from .identity and .models. TestModulePurity uses ast.parse to walk every import in models.py and __init__.py and assert no name starts with 'nats_core', 'nats', 'langgraph', or 'forge.adapters'—the AST-based check would catch even a transitive import that ruff would miss.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: ruff check src/forge/gating/models.py src/forge/gating/__init__.py tests/forge/gating/test_models.py → 'All checks passed!'. ruff format --check on the same set → '3 files already formatted'. (Pre-existing identity.py / test_identity.py format diffs are out of scope for this task.)
